questions.question: Explain the background and evolution of server-side web technologies. How did Servlets improve upon CGI?
keyPoints: Early web: static HTML pages served by web servers
CGI introduced dynamic content but with process-per-request overhead
Servlets introduced thread-based model with JVM persistence
Better performance, scalability, and platform independence
Access to full Java ecosystem and built-in session management

questions.question: What is a servlet container? Explain its role in handling HTTP requests.
keyPoints: Container manages servlet lifecycle (load, init, service, destroy)
Maps incoming URLs to appropriate servlets
Creates request and response objects
Manages threading for concurrent requests
Examples: Tomcat, Jetty, GlassFish

questions.question: Compare and contrast CGI and Servlet technologies with respect to performance, scalability, and ease of development.
keyPoints: CGI: new process per request vs Servlet: thread per request
CGI: language-dependent vs Servlet: platform-independent Java
CGI: no built-in session management vs Servlet: HttpSession API
Servlet: persistent in memory between requests
Servlet: access to full Java API and libraries

questions.question: Describe the HTTP request-response model and how servlets fit into this architecture.
keyPoints: Client sends HTTP request with method, headers, body
Web server receives and routes to servlet container
Container creates request/response objects and invokes servlet
Servlet processes request and writes response
Response sent back to client with status code, headers, body
